framework.reasoning: Paper does not name a framework explicitly, but the GAT architecture (Velickovic et al. 2017) with custom edge-aware attention maps directly onto PyTorch Geometric's MessagePassing API, the domain convention for GNN research code. YAML chosen over Hydra/OmegaConf for a self-contained, dependency-light config system since the paper's hyperparameter grid (Table 8-10) is flat and does not require hierarchical composition.

module_hierarchy.description: Edge-aware GAT layer implementing the modified attention mechanism of Sec. 5 (SIR modules: GATLayer, MultiHeadAttention).

public_methods.name: __init__
public_methods.signature: (in_dim: int, out_dim: int, edge_dim: int, heads: int = 4, concat: bool = True, negative_slope: float = 0.1, dropout: float = 0.1, attn_dropout: float = 0.1)

public_methods.name: forward
public_methods.signature: (x: torch.Tensor, edge_index: torch.Tensor, edge_attr: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor

module_hierarchy.description: Full SpotV2Net model: stacks EdgeAwareGATLayer modules per the tuned 2-hidden-layer architecture and applies the final linear prediction head (SIR modules: GATStack, PredictionHead). Supports a `use_edge_features` flag to reproduce the SpotV2Net-NE ablation.

public_methods.name: __init__
public_methods.signature: (node_in_dim: int, edge_in_dim: int, hidden_dims: list, heads: int = 4, output_dim: int = 1, use_edge_features: bool = True, dropout: float = 0.1, attn_dropout: float = 0.1, negative_slope: float = 0.1)

public_methods.name: forward
public_methods.signature: (x: torch.Tensor, edge_index: torch.Tensor, edge_attr: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor

module_hierarchy.description: Panel HAR-Spot baseline (App. A.1) implemented as a linear regression over engineered lag-average features.
LSTM baseline (App. A.3) accepting only volatility/co-volatility sequences (no vol-of-vol), per the paper's stated input restriction.

public_methods.name: __init__
public_methods.signature: (input_dim: int, hidden_dims: list, output_dim: int = 1, dropout: float = 0.3)

module_hierarchy.description: Non-parametric Fourier (Malliavin-Mancino) estimators for spot volatility, co-volatility, volatility-of-volatility, co-volatility-of-volatility (Sec. 6). Flagged low-confidence for exact cutting-frequency reproduction (SIR ambiguity).

public_methods.name: estimate_volatility
public_methods.signature: (log_returns: np.ndarray, Nv: int, Mv: int, grid: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray

public_methods.name: estimate_covolatility
public_methods.signature: (log_returns_i: np.ndarray, log_returns_j: np.ndarray, Nc: int, Mc: int, grid: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray

public_methods.name: estimate_vol_of_vol
public_methods.signature: (log_returns: np.ndarray, Nv: int, Sv: int, Lv: int, grid: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray

public_methods.name: estimate_covol_of_vol
public_methods.signature: (log_returns_i: np.ndarray, log_returns_j: np.ndarray, Nc: int, Sc: int, Lc: int, grid: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray

module_hierarchy.description: Builds per-timestamp fully-connected graph tensors (node/edge feature matrices with lag stacking) from Fourier estimate time series (SIR module: InputGraphConstruction, Eq. 1-2).

public_methods.name: build_node_features
public_methods.signature: (vol_df: 'pandas.DataFrame', covol_df: 'pandas.DataFrame', num_lags: int, timestamp_idx: int) -> np.ndarray

public_methods.name: build_edge_features
public_methods.signature: (vov_df: 'pandas.DataFrame', covov_df: 'pandas.DataFrame', num_lags: int, timestamp_idx: int) -> np.ndarray

public_methods.signature: (data_dir: str, split: str, num_lags: int, horizon: int = 1, use_edge_features: bool = True)

module_hierarchy.description: Feature standardization (fit on train split only) and jump-filtering (Sec. 7.1 footnote 7, beta=0.5, alpha=0.5).

public_methods.name: filter
public_methods.signature: (returns: np.ndarray, T: float, n: int, beta: float = 0.5, alpha: float = 0.5) -> np.ndarray

module_hierarchy.description: Training loop: epoch iteration, checkpointing (best validation MSE), logging, seed control, dry-run/debug support.
MSE, QLIKE aggregate metrics; Diebold-Mariano test; Model Confidence Set wrapper (Sec. 7.2).
GNNExplainer wrapper for per-node subgraph interpretation (Sec. 7.3).

public_methods.name: explain_node
public_methods.signature: (model: 'torch.nn.Module', graph: dict, node_idx: int, top_k: int = 5) -> dict

module_hierarchy.description: YAML config loading, validation, and global seeding (Python/NumPy/PyTorch).

tensor_flows.name: SpotV2Net forward pass (single-step)
steps: x: [30, M] float32 <- node features (contemporaneous + L lags of vol/co-vol)
edge_attr: [30*29, E] float32 <- edge features (contemporaneous + L lags of vol-of-vol/co-vol-of-vol)
x = EdgeAwareGATLayer_1(x, edge_index, edge_attr) -> [30, K*H1] (concat heads), ReLU
x = EdgeAwareGATLayer_2(x, edge_index, edge_attr_proj) -> [30, H2] (heads averaged), ReLU

tensor_flows.name: SpotV2Net forward pass (multi-step / functional)
steps: Identical GAT stack to single-step variant
y_hat = Linear(H2 -> 14)(x) -> [30, 14], joint (non-recursive) forecast over the next trading day's 30-min grid

model.name: spotv2net
model.node_in_dim: computed from num_lags and num_assets at runtime

training.beta1: 0.9  # ASSUMED: PyTorch default, not specified in paper
training.beta2: 0.999  # ASSUMED: PyTorch default, not specified in paper
training.weight_decay: 0.01  # ASSUMED: PyTorch default, not specified in paper
training.lr_schedule: none  # ASSUMED: no scheduler mentioned in Table 8
training.gradient_clip_norm: null  # ASSUMED: not mentioned in paper

runtime.reason: GAT layer base classes and graph batching utilities
numerical arrays and Fourier coefficient computation
time series handling for volatility/co-volatility panels

entrypoints.purpose: Train SpotV2Net (or a baseline) on the DJIA graph dataset

cli_args.help: Reduce dataset/steps for quick local testing

entrypoints.purpose: Evaluate a trained checkpoint on the validation/test split, computing MSE/QLIKE/MCS/DM-test
Run single-timestamp forecast given a config, checkpoint, and a graph snapshot

cli_args.help: Path to a single graph snapshot (npz/json)

risk_assessment.description: Exact Fourier cutting-frequency hyperparameters (Nc, Mc, Sc, Lc, Nvi, Mvi, Svi, Lvi) needed for numerically faithful spot volatility/vol-of-vol estimation are deferred to an external MATLAB library (Sanfelici & Toscano, 2024) and not given in the paper.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement FourierSpotEstimator as fully configurable; ship literature-typical defaults; add a clear README_data.md note directing users to the FMVol library or Mancino & Recchioni (2015) asymptotic MSE-minimizing frequency formulas for exact reproduction.

risk_assessment.description: Raw 1-second TAQ tick data from WRDS is proprietary and not redistributable.
risk_assessment.mitigation: data/download.py checks for a user-supplied WRDS extract; ships a synthetic data generator for smoke-testing the pipeline without real data.

risk_assessment.description: LR schedule, gradient clipping, and AdamW beta/weight-decay values are not disclosed (SIR training_pipeline confidence 0.65).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Expose all as config-driven with '# ASSUMED' comments; default to PyTorch AdamW defaults and no scheduler/clipping, easily overridden.

risk_assessment.description: Exact interaction between multi-head concatenation and the tuned hidden dims [400,200]/[400,400] is ambiguous (per-head dim vs. total post-concat dim).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Model class accepts hidden_dims as the *total* output dimension per layer and internally divides by heads when concat=True, matching the more standard PyG GATConv convention; documented via docstring with the ambiguity noted.

risk_assessment.description: GNNExplainer's exact optimization hyperparameters (epochs, LR for the mask optimization) are not given.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use PyG's GNNExplainer default optimization settings; document as an assumption in explainer.py docstring.
